root_text.an1.575:1.1: ‚ÄúYassa kassaci, bhikkhave, mahńĀsamuddo cetasńĀ phuŠĻ≠o antogadhńĀ tassa kunnadiyo yńĀ kńĀci samuddaŠĻÖgamńĀ;
root_text.an1.575:1.2: evamevaŠĻĀ, bhikkhave, yassa kassaci kńĀyagatńĀ sati bhńĀvitńĀ bahulńękatńĀ antogadhńĀ tassa kusalńĀ dhammńĀ ye keci vijjńĀbhńĀgiyńĀ‚ÄĚti.
root_text.an1.576-582:1.1: ‚ÄúEkadhammo, bhikkhave, bhńĀvito bahulńękato mahato saŠĻĀvegńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ mahato atthńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ mahato yogakkhemńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ satisampaja√Ī√ĪńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ √ĪńĀŠĻáadassanappaŠĻ≠ilńĀbhńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ diŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠hadhammasukhavihńĀrńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ vijjńĀvimuttiphalasacchikiriyńĀya saŠĻĀvattati.
root_text.an1.576-582:1.4: AyaŠĻĀ kho, bhikkhave, ekadhammo bhńĀvito bahulńękato mahato saŠĻĀvegńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ mahato atthńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ mahato yogakkhemńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ satisampaja√Ī√ĪńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ √ĪńĀŠĻáadassanappaŠĻ≠ilńĀbhńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ diŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠hadhammasukhavihńĀrńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ vijjńĀvimuttiphalasacchikiriyńĀya saŠĻĀvattatńę‚ÄĚti.
root_text.an1.583:1.1: ‚ÄúEkadhamme, bhikkhave, bhńĀvite bahulńękate kńĀyopi passambhati, cittampi passambhati, vitakkavicńĀrńĀpi vŇępasammanti, kevalńĀpi vijjńĀbhńĀgiyńĀ dhammńĀ bhńĀvanńĀpńĀripŇęriŠĻĀ gacchanti.
root_text.an1.583:1.4: ImasmiŠĻĀ kho, bhikkhave, ekadhamme bhńĀvite bahulńękate kńĀyopi passambhati, cittampi passambhati, vitakkavicńĀrńĀpi vŇępasammanti, kevalńĀpi vijjńĀbhńĀgiyńĀ dhammńĀ bhńĀvanńĀpńĀripŇęriŠĻĀ gacchantńę‚ÄĚti.
root_text.an1.584:1.1: ‚ÄúEkadhamme, bhikkhave, bhńĀvite bahulńękate anuppannńĀ ceva akusalńĀ dhammńĀ nuppajjanti, uppannńĀ ca akusalńĀ dhammńĀ pahńęyanti.
root_text.an1.584:1.4: ImasmiŠĻĀ kho, bhikkhave, ekadhamme bhńĀvite bahulńękate anuppannńĀ ceva akusalńĀ dhammńĀ nuppajjanti, uppannńĀ ca akusalńĀ dhammńĀ pahńęyantńę‚ÄĚti.
root_text.an1.585:1.1: ‚ÄúEkadhamme, bhikkhave, bhńĀvite bahulńękate anuppannńĀ ceva kusalńĀ dhammńĀ uppajjanti, uppannńĀ ca kusalńĀ dhammńĀ bhiyyobhńĀvńĀya vepullńĀya saŠĻĀvattanti.
root_text.an1.585:1.4: ImasmiŠĻĀ kho, bhikkhave, ekadhamme bhńĀvite bahulńękate anuppannńĀ ceva kusalńĀ dhammńĀ uppajjanti, uppannńĀ ca kusalńĀ dhammńĀ bhiyyobhńĀvńĀya vepullńĀya saŠĻĀvattantńę‚ÄĚti.
root_text.an1.586-590:1.1: ‚ÄúEkadhamme, bhikkhave, bhńĀvite bahulńękate avijjńĀ pahńęyati ‚Ä¶ vijjńĀ uppajjati ‚Ä¶ asmimńĀno pahńęyati ‚Ä¶ anusayńĀ samugghńĀtaŠĻĀ gacchanti ‚Ä¶ saŠĻĀyojanńĀ pahńęyanti.
root_text.an1.586-590:1.4: ImasmiŠĻĀ kho, bhikkhave, ekadhamme bhńĀvite bahulńękate avijjńĀ pahńęyati ‚Ä¶ vijjńĀ uppajjati ‚Ä¶ asmimńĀno pahńęyati ‚Ä¶ anusayńĀ samugghńĀtaŠĻĀ gacchanti ‚Ä¶ saŠĻĀyojanńĀ pahńęyantńę‚ÄĚti.
root_text.an1.591-592:1.1: ‚ÄúEkadhammo, bhikkhave, bhńĀvito bahulńękato pa√Ī√ĪńĀpabhedńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ anupńĀdńĀparinibbńĀnńĀya saŠĻĀvattati.
root_text.an1.591-592:1.4: AyaŠĻĀ kho, bhikkhave, ekadhammo bhńĀvito bahulńękato pa√Ī√ĪńĀpabhedńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ anupńĀdńĀparinibbńĀnńĀya saŠĻĀvattatńę‚ÄĚti.
root_text.an1.593-595:1.1: ‚ÄúEkadhamme, bhikkhave, bhńĀvite bahulńękate anekadhńĀtupaŠĻ≠ivedho hoti ‚Ä¶ nńĀnńĀdhńĀtupaŠĻ≠ivedho hoti ‚Ä¶ anekadhńĀtupaŠĻ≠isambhidńĀ hoti.
root_text.an1.593-595:1.4: ImasmiŠĻĀ kho, bhikkhave, ekadhamme bhńĀvite bahulńękate anekadhńĀtupaŠĻ≠ivedho hoti ‚Ä¶ nńĀnńĀdhńĀtupaŠĻ≠ivedho hoti ‚Ä¶ anekadhńĀtupaŠĻ≠isambhidńĀ hotńę‚ÄĚti.
root_text.an1.596-599:1.1: ‚ÄúEkadhammo, bhikkhave, bhńĀvito bahulńękato sotńĀpattiphalasacchikiriyńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ sakadńĀgńĀmiphalasacchikiriyńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ anńĀgńĀmiphalasacchikiriyńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ arahattaphalasacchikiriyńĀya saŠĻĀvattati.
root_text.an1.596-599:1.4: AyaŠĻĀ kho, bhikkhave, ekadhammo bhńĀvito bahulńękato sotńĀpattiphalasacchikiriyńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ sakadńĀgńĀmiphalasacchikiriyńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ anńĀgńĀmiphalasacchikiriyńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ arahattaphalasacchikiriyńĀya saŠĻĀvattatńę‚ÄĚti.
root_text.an1.600-615:1.1: ‚ÄúEkadhammo, bhikkhave, bhńĀvito bahulńękato pa√Ī√ĪńĀpaŠĻ≠ilńĀbhńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ pa√Ī√ĪńĀvuddhiyńĀ saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ pa√Ī√ĪńĀvepullńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ mahńĀpa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ puthupa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ vipulapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ gambhńęrapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ asńĀmantapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ bhŇęripa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ pa√Ī√ĪńĀbńĀhullńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ sńęghapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ lahupa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ hńĀsapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ javanapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ tikkhapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ nibbedhikapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati.
root_text.an1.600-615:1.4: AyaŠĻĀ kho, bhikkhave, ekadhammo bhńĀvito bahulńękato pa√Ī√ĪńĀpaŠĻ≠ilńĀbhńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ pa√Ī√ĪńĀvuddhiyńĀ saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ pa√Ī√ĪńĀvepullńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ mahńĀpa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ puthupa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ vipulapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ gambhńęrapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ asńĀmantapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ bhŇęripa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ pa√Ī√ĪńĀbńĀhullńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ sńęghapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ lahupa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ hńĀsapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ javanapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ tikkhapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattati ‚Ä¶ nibbedhikapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya saŠĻĀvattatńę‚ÄĚti.

translation_text.an1.575:0.2: The Chapter on Mindfulness of the Body
translation_text.an1.575:1.1: ‚ÄúMendicants, anyone who brings into their mind the great ocean includes all of the streams that run into it.
translation_text.an1.575:1.2: In the same way, anyone who has developed and cultivated mindfulness of the body includes all of the skillful qualities that play a part in realization.‚ÄĚ
translation_text.an1.576-582:1.1: ‚ÄúOne thing, mendicants, when developed and cultivated leads to great urgency ‚Ä¶ great benefit ‚Ä¶ great sanctuary from the yoke ‚Ä¶ mindfulness and awareness ‚Ä¶ gaining knowledge and vision ‚Ä¶ blissful meditation in this life ‚Ä¶ the realization of the fruit of knowledge and freedom.
translation_text.an1.576-582:1.4: This one thing, when developed and cultivated, leads to great urgency ‚Ä¶ great benefit ‚Ä¶ great sanctuary from the yoke ‚Ä¶ mindfulness and awareness ‚Ä¶ gaining knowledge and vision ‚Ä¶ blissful meditation in this life ‚Ä¶ the realization of the fruit of knowledge and freedom.‚ÄĚ
translation_text.an1.583:1.1: ‚ÄúWhen one thing, mendicants, is developed and cultivated the body and mind become tranquil, thinking and considering settle down, and all of the qualities that play a part in realization are fully developed.
translation_text.an1.583:1.4: When this one thing is developed and cultivated, the body and mind become tranquil, thinking and considering settle down, and all of the qualities that play a part in realization are fully developed.‚ÄĚ
translation_text.an1.584:1.1: ‚ÄúWhen one thing, mendicants, is developed and cultivated, unskillful qualities do not arise, and, if they‚Äôve already arisen, they are given up.
translation_text.an1.584:1.4: When this one thing is developed and cultivated, unskillful qualities do not arise, and, if they‚Äôve already arisen, they are given up.‚ÄĚ
translation_text.an1.585:1.1: ‚ÄúWhen one thing, mendicants, is developed and cultivated, skillful qualities arise, and, once they‚Äôve arisen, they increase and grow.
translation_text.an1.585:1.4: When this one thing is developed and cultivated, skillful qualities arise, and, once they‚Äôve arisen, they increase and grow.‚ÄĚ
translation_text.an1.586-590:1.1: ‚ÄúWhen one thing, mendicants, is developed and cultivated, ignorance is given up ‚Ä¶ knowledge arises ‚Ä¶ the conceit ‚ÄėI am‚Äô is given up ‚Ä¶ the underlying tendencies are uprooted ‚Ä¶ the fetters are given up.
translation_text.an1.586-590:1.4: When this one thing is developed and cultivated, ignorance is given up ‚Ä¶ knowledge arises ‚Ä¶ the conceit ‚ÄėI am‚Äô is given up ‚Ä¶ the underlying tendencies are uprooted ‚Ä¶ the fetters are given up.‚ÄĚ
translation_text.an1.591-592:1.1: ‚ÄúOne thing, mendicants, when developed and cultivated leads to demolition by wisdom ‚Ä¶ to complete extinguishment with no fuel for grasping.
translation_text.an1.591-592:1.4: This one thing, mendicants, when developed and cultivated leads to demolition by wisdom ‚Ä¶ to complete extinguishment with no fuel for grasping.‚ÄĚ
translation_text.an1.593-595:1.1: ‚ÄúWhen one thing is developed and cultivated there is the penetration of many elements ‚Ä¶ the penetration of diverse elements ‚Ä¶ the analysis of many elements.
translation_text.an1.593-595:1.4: When this one thing is developed and cultivated there is the penetration of many elements ‚Ä¶ the penetration of diverse elements ‚Ä¶ the analysis of many elements.‚ÄĚ
translation_text.an1.596-599:1.1: ‚ÄúOne thing, mendicants, when developed and cultivated leads to the realization of the fruit of stream-entry ‚Ä¶ once-return ‚Ä¶ non-return ‚Ä¶ perfection.
translation_text.an1.596-599:1.4: This one thing, when developed and cultivated, leads to the realization of the fruit of stream-entry ‚Ä¶ once-return ‚Ä¶ non-return ‚Ä¶ perfection.‚ÄĚ
translation_text.an1.600-615:1.1: ‚ÄúOne thing, mendicants, when developed and cultivated, leads to the getting of wisdom ‚Ä¶ the growth of wisdom ‚Ä¶ the increase of wisdom ‚Ä¶ to great wisdom ‚Ä¶ to widespread wisdom ‚Ä¶ to abundant wisdom ‚Ä¶ to deep wisdom ‚Ä¶ to extraordinary wisdom ‚Ä¶ to vast wisdom ‚Ä¶ to much wisdom ‚Ä¶ to fast wisdom ‚Ä¶ to light wisdom ‚Ä¶ to laughing wisdom ‚Ä¶ to swift wisdom ‚Ä¶ to sharp wisdom ‚Ä¶ to penetrating wisdom.
translation_text.an1.600-615:1.4: This one thing, when developed and cultivated, leads to the getting of wisdom ‚Ä¶ the growth of wisdom ‚Ä¶ the increase of wisdom ‚Ä¶ to great wisdom ‚Ä¶ to widespread wisdom ‚Ä¶ to abundant wisdom ‚Ä¶ to deep wisdom ‚Ä¶ to extraordinary wisdom ‚Ä¶ to vast wisdom ‚Ä¶ to much wisdom ‚Ä¶ to fast wisdom ‚Ä¶ to light wisdom ‚Ä¶ to laughing wisdom ‚Ä¶ to swift wisdom ‚Ä¶ to sharp wisdom ‚Ä¶ to penetrating wisdom.‚ÄĚ

variant_text.an1.600-615:1.1: asńĀmantapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya ‚Üí asamatthapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya (sya-all, km); asamattapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya (mr); asamantapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya (ŠĻ≠ńękńĀ) | hńĀsapa√Ī√ĪatńĀya ‚Üí hńĀsupa√Ī√ĪatńĀya (bj, pts1ed)

reference_text.an1.583:1.1: cck20.56, csp1ed15.45, csp2ed15.45, ms15A1_460, msdiv571, ndp16.43, pts-vp-pli1.44.1
